input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's the full setup:

**AppShell.xaml**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Views"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard (single page, shown in flyout) -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**

```csharp
namespace MyMauiApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register routes for pages not in the visual hierarchy
        Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating to the details page** (e.g. from `ActiveProductsPage`):

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails", new Dictionary<string, object>
{
    ["Product"] = selectedProduct
});
```

**Receiving the parameter** in `ProductDetailsPage`:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(Product), "Product")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public Product Product { get; set; }
}
```

### Key points

| Concept | How it maps |
|---|---|
| **FlyoutItem** | Top-level flyout entry |
| **Tab > ShellContent** | Sub-tabs under a single flyout entry |
| **Routing.RegisterRoute** | For pages outside the Shell visual tree (detail pages) |
| **ContentTemplate** | Lazy-loads pages on first navigation |

The `productdetails` route is relative, so navigating with `"productdetails"` pushes it onto the current navigation stack — the back button returns to whichever products tab the user was on.

2. A Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for a ProductDetailsPage
